release-date.comment: Announced February 2021, became publicly available in June 2021.

status.comment: Both 4GB and 8GB variants listed in stock at the Pine Store as of 2026-04-26.

open-source-hardware.comment: Pine64 publishes the schematic and PCB placement as PDFs only — no Gerbers or native PCB design files released, and no OSHW-compatible license is stated. RESEARCH.md guideline: PDF schematics alone do not qualify as OSHW.

country-of-origin.comment: Pine64 is headquartered in Fremont, California; boards are manufactured in China.

vulkan-support.comment: Mali-G52 2EE supports Vulkan 1.1 per vendor specs; mainline panfrost driver also exposes Vulkan-class features.

npu-performance-tops.comment: Vendor-quoted INT8 peak. RK3566's NPU is small (0.8 TOPS) compared with later Rockchip SoCs.

hw-video-decode.comment: RK3566 4K@60 decode for H.264, H.265, VP9 (and AVS2). No hardware AV1 decode on RK3566.

hw-video-encode.comment: 1080p@60 H.264/H.265 hardware encode via Rockchip MPP.

ram-min-gb.comment: A 2 GB SKU was discussed pre-launch but not sold; Pine Store ships only 4 GB and 8 GB variants of Model A.

emmc.comment: eMMC module socket (8 GB–128 GB). Module sold separately.

m2-nvme-slot.comment: Model A uses an open-ended PCIe x4 slot (PCIe 2.0 x1 electrically) rather than M.2. Model B has M.2.

sata.comment: Native SATA 3.0 6 Gb/s port; mutually exclusive with the USB 3.0 port (shared PHY).

ethernet.comment: Single 10/100/1000 Mbps RJ45; iperf3 sustains >910 Mbit/s per vendor.

wifi.comment: Wi-Fi is optional on Model A (sold separately as a module); 802.11ac is Wi-Fi 5. The bare board ships without wireless.

bluetooth.comment: Provided by the optional Wi-Fi/BT module; bare board has no Bluetooth.

usb-a-3x-ports.comment: 1× USB 3.0 host port (shares PHY with the SATA port — only one can be active).

usb-a-2-ports.comment: Counts as 3 USB 2.0 host ports per CNX-Software; the wiki separates this into 2× USB 2.0 host plus 1× USB 2.0 OTG, but all are USB-A.

usb-c-ports-data.comment: Power is delivered via DC barrel jack; no USB-C ports on Model A.

displayport.comment: 4-lane embedded DisplayPort (eDP) connector up to 2560×1600@60. Not a standard full-size DP socket — physically eDP only.

audio-jack.comment: 3.5 mm stereo headphone jack with mic input.

gpio-pins.comment: 2×10-pin (= 20 pins) header on Model A. Model B uses 2×20.

rpi-compatible-header.comment: Header is 2×10 (20-pin) — not pin-compatible with the 40-pin RPi GPIO.

rpi-hat-compatible.comment: Smaller header and different mounting hole pattern preclude HAT compatibility.

pcie-lanes-exposed.comment: Mechanically a ×4 open-ended PCIe slot, electrically a single PCIe 2.0 lane (RK3566 limit).

i2c-spi-uart.comment: I²C, SPI, and UART available on the GPIO header.

power-input.comment: Center-positive 5.5/2.1 mm barrel jack; lithium battery connector with thermistor also fitted.

idle-power-w.comment: Vendor/community report: <2 W at idle for the bare board (forum, 2021). No independent third-party Phoronix-style measurement located, so treat as approximate.

load-power-w.comment: Forum-reported <5 W under stress-ng -c4 on Model B (same SoC). No published third-party measurement specifically for Model A; figure is approximate.

cooling-required.comment: RK3566 with quad A55 at 1.8 GHz is a low-power SoC; vendor ships no cooler and the board operates passively at typical loads.

rtc-battery-header.comment: JST-PH RTC battery connector on board.

form-factor-class.comment: 133×80 mm PCB; not Pi-standard, not Pico/Nano-ITX. Custom Pine64 form factor.

weight-g.comment: Pine Store lists shipping/board weight as 0.110 kg.

mainline-linux-kernel.comment: Mainline-first community: Linux 6.1+ supports the board with most peripherals (Ethernet, USB, HDMI, GPIO, SATA, PCIe). U-Boot mainline supports Quartz64 since v2023.10-rc2. NPU and the rkvdec2 video decoder still lack mainline drivers, but those are SoC-wide gaps not specific to this board.

official-os-image.comment: Pine64 does not publish a vendor-maintained Linux image; the wiki points to community images (Armbian, Manjaro ARM, DietPi, Plebian) instead.

distros-with-images.comment: Armbian, Manjaro ARM, DietPi, and Plebian (Debian Bookworm-based) publish board-specific images. Ubuntu/Fedora/openSUSE/Alpine do not have official Quartz64 images. Listed Debian via Plebian since it is the documented Debian path; the project is community-run.

bootloader.comment: Mainline U-Boot supports Quartz64 from v2023.10-rc2; an EDK2 (UEFI) port also exists for NetBSD/UEFI workflows.

long-term-support.comment: Pine64 publishes no formal LTS window. Community/mainline kernel adoption means support effectively tracks upstream Linux for this SoC, but vendor-side guarantees are not documented.

android-support.comment: Android 11 and Android 13 SDK builds available, including eMMC production-test images.

msrp-base-usd.comment: Pine Store price for 4 GB Model A as of 2026-04-26.

msrp-top-usd.comment: Pine Store price for 8 GB Model A as of 2026-04-26.

available-regions.comment: Pine Store ships globally; resellers (Botland in EU, etc.) also stock the board.

typical-lead-time.comment: Both 4 GB and 8 GB SKUs marked in stock at the Pine Store on 2026-04-26.

documentation-quality.comment: Pine64 wiki + documentation portal + published PDF schematic + active forum. Lacks a polished vendor getting-started guide compared to Raspberry Pi but is comprehensive for a community-driven board.

community-size.comment: Pine64 is an established second-tier vendor with an active forum, Discord, and broad ecosystem (PinePhone, ROCKPro64, etc.).

active-forum-discord.comment: Pine64 forum (forum.pine64.org) and Pine64 Discord/Matrix are vendor-blessed and active.

accessory-ecosystem.comment: Pine64 sells eMMC modules, Wi-Fi/BT modules, an acrylic case, and a few accessories. No HAT ecosystem (incompatible header) and limited third-party add-ons. Sparse compared with RPi but better than truly niche boards.
